metadata.source: NRC ISFSI Licensing, Federal Register, NRC Exemption Documents
known_gaps: Per-site cask counts and tonnage not published by NRC for general-license ISFSIs
General-license ISFSIs have no independent license expiration — authorized under reactor Part 50 operating license
metadata.notes: Morris Operation is the only licensed commercial away-from-reactor wet spent fuel storage facility in the US. All six operating plant sites have general-license ISFSIs under 10 CFR 72 Subpart K. Plant-site ISFSI coordinates are offset slightly from reactor centroids for map legibility.

properties.name: Morris Operation (GE-Hitachi ISFSI)
properties.notes: Only licensed commercial away-from-reactor spent fuel storage facility in the US. Stores 772 metric tons of spent fuel. License renewed 2022.

properties.name: Zion ISFSI
properties.notes: Spent fuel remains on site after plant decommissioning completed 2023.

properties.name: Braidwood ISFSI
properties.notes: Co-located with Braidwood Generating Station. General license under 10 CFR 72 Subpart K. Per-site cask count and tonnage not published by NRC.

properties.name: Byron ISFSI
properties.notes: Co-located with Byron Generating Station. General license under 10 CFR 72 Subpart K. Per-site cask count and tonnage not published by NRC.

properties.name: Clinton ISFSI
properties.notes: Co-located with Clinton Power Station. General license under 10 CFR 72 Subpart K. Per-site cask count and tonnage not published by NRC.

properties.name: Dresden ISFSI
properties.notes: Co-located with Dresden Generating Station. Separate from the adjacent Morris Operation (72-0001) away-from-reactor facility. General license under 10 CFR 72 Subpart K. NRC exemption documents (2024) confirm multiple MPC-68M-CBS canisters loaded.

properties.name: LaSalle ISFSI
properties.notes: Co-located with LaSalle County Generating Station. General license under 10 CFR 72 Subpart K. Per-site cask count and tonnage not published by NRC.

properties.name: Quad Cities ISFSI
properties.notes: Co-located with Quad Cities Generating Station. General license under 10 CFR 72 Subpart K. Per-site cask count and tonnage not published by NRC.
